step_by_step_thinking:
From the articles reviewed, we understand that MHC antigens, specifically HLA class I antigens, are typically present on almost every nucleated cell except mature red blood cells (erythrocytes). Since the question asks about what MHC antigens are absent on, the correct option would be erythrocytes.
\boxed{B} Based on the information given, MHC antigens like HLA class I are notably absent on erythrocytes while being present on nearly all other nucleated cells listed (neutrophils, monocytes, etc.).
